📋 LTX-2 Native Resolutions

All values are divisible by 32 to ensure compatibility.

Standard Landscape (16:9)

	832 x 480 (480p)
	960 x 520 (540p)
    1280 x 704 (720p optimized)
    1920 x 1088 (Native 1080p)
    2560 x 1440 (2K - High VRAM)

Portrait / Social (9:16)

    704 x 1280 (Standard Vertical)
    1088 x 1920 (High Res Vertical)
	
Cinematic Widescreen (2.39:1)

    1536 x 640 (Efficient Wide)
    1920 x 800 (Full Wide)

Square (1:1)

    768 x 768 (Fast)
    1024 x 1024 (Standard)

⚠️ Performance Note: Since you are rendering natively (no upscale), 1920x1088 uses significantly more VRAM than the default workflow. If you get OOM errors, reduce your Frame Count (Length) to roughly 49-65 frames.

a man with a blue painted face is speaking while the crowd around him are agitated

a man with a blue painted face is speaking while the crowd around him are agitated. 

Someone hands the man a sword that he takes and holds in the air as he continues to speak.

a man with a blue painted face is speaking while the crowd around him are agitated. 

Someone hands the man a bow of steaming hot chinese food, he swaps the sword in his hand for the bowl of food.
The crowd behind him look restless, they wish they had something to eat.

MARKDKBERRY DOT COM:

23rd January 2026

***This workflow is for extending an image using audio-in file to drive the video lipsync. It does now include NAG for overall control, and positive prompts to drive activity in each new group extension.***

*For extending an existing video see other extender workflows*

---

## TESTING 720p WITH MEM NODES & NAG:

*On 3060 RTX 12 GB VRAM with 32 GB System Ram*.

Testing 28 second audio (succulent chinese) with Braveheart but using the Memory efficient nodes to get it to 720p. (couldnt get above 480p with this before).

first set: 13 mins (completed it, it fell over on the 2nd set at 720p before).

second set: 13 mins (so far looking to be holding well GPU is unchanged and below 14% blap).
annoyingly it didnt present it in the video combine node which has been happening a lot but its onto the last one...

third set: 13 mins (so this is good, it is fixing the VRAM each set and then continuing the mem efficient nodes work well).

FINAL RESULT: It did actually OOM on the last VAE but hitting run again finished it. all done in 40 mins, 28 seconds 720p and worked well. The issue was it wasnt showing the output in Video Combine nodes but right click and save preview was saving them. 

---

## MISC NOTES:

it does OOM on last VAE decode but hit run again and it should finish without starting over.

The video combine nodes dont always show the results but right click and save preview if they havent done so (esp the last one in the line).

For some reason after fresh restart the first group overloaded VRAM and slowed down to 15 mins (usually 13) but the next set was back to full speed and ended fine.

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
